title: Divider
description: A divider creates visual separation between related content.
whenToUse: To separate groups of related content. Between sections within a card or panel.
whenNotToUse: When whitespace alone provides sufficient separation. Do not use as decoration.
acceptanceCriteria: Renders at all four padding sizes (sm/md/lg/xl)
Border color uses --uds-color-border-secondary so it adapts to light/dark/brand modes
When grouping is meaningful, markup includes role="separator"

props.name: data-padding
props.description: Vertical padding around the rule. Use larger paddings to add breathing room between major sections.

states.name: default
states.description: Static horizontal rule; no interactive states

states.name: not-applicable
states.description: Divider is not focusable or interactive

contentGuidelines: Dividers carry no text. Communicate grouping through the divider's position and the surrounding heading hierarchy, not the divider itself.

dos: Use role="separator" (or a semantic <hr>) when the divider conveys content grouping
Pick a padding size proportional to the surrounding content density
donts: Don't use a divider when whitespace alone communicates separation
Don't use dividers as decoration; they should always indicate a content boundary
Don't stack multiple dividers — increase padding instead

keyboard.action: Not focusable; the divider is presentational

screenReader.trigger: Encountered in reading order with role="separator"
screenReader.announcement: Most screen readers ignore decorative dividers; when role=separator is set, they announce a structural break

wcag.howMet: Use role="separator" or a semantic <hr> when the divider conveys grouping; omit role for purely decorative spacing

_legacyAccessibilityNotes: Purely presentational — use <hr> for semantic separation if appropriate, otherwise role="separator".
